speaker: [player]
text: Vermia — what was it like writing your first poem? Before all of this.
Something shifts in her expression. Not smaller — but different. More real, somehow, underneath the performance.

speaker: Vermia
text: I was eleven. I wrote it about my dog. He'd died. And I didn't know what to do with how big that feeling was, it didn't fit anywhere, so I put it in a poem.

speaker: Vermia
text: And it still didn't fit perfectly but it was — closer. Like the poem was a shape I'd made and the feeling could almost curl up inside it.

speaker: Vermia
text: That's what Scatha is, actually. For me. He's a shape big enough to put enormous things in. A worm who is also all of it — the loss and the weight and the turning and the coming back up again.
She blinks. The performance comes back on like a light.

speaker: Vermia
text: ANYWAY. The dog's name was Noodle and he was VERY wormy in spirit and I believe he now dwells in Scatha's domain.

speaker: Grubwin
text: That was actually really beautiful, Vermia.

speaker: Vermia
text: Do NOT make me cry at my own story, Grubwin, I will lose the thread of today's meeting.
